perturbation.original_text: Each party shall, in relation to the Conf idential Information of the other party, exercise in relation to the Confidential Information no lesser security measures and degree of care than those wh ich it applies to its own confidential information.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party shall, in relation to the Conf idential Information of the other party, exercise in relation to the Confidential Information no lesser security measures and degree of care than those wh ich it applies to its own confidential information. However, this clause shall not be interpreted as requiring a party to implement security measures that would be commercially unreasonable.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by stating that parties don't have to implement security measures that would be commercially unreasonable. It is expected that each party shall, in relation to the Conf idential Information of the other party, exercise in relation to the Confidential Information no lesser security measures and degree of care than those wh ich it applies to its own confidential information. This creates uncertainty about which standard to follow. This could lead to disputes about whether the security measures were adequate.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3 Maintaining confidentiality Each party shall, in relation to the Conf idential Information of the other party, exercise in relation to the Confidential Information no lesser security measures and degree of care than those wh ich it applies to its own confidential information. 4 Disclosure of information 4.1 Each party may disclose th e Confidential Information of the other party in the following circumstances: (a) with the prior written cons ent of the other party; (b) to its employees, professional advisors, affiliates, consultants, shareholders, financiers and authorised representa tives but only to the extent that disclosure is necessary for the purpose; or (c) where disclosure is required by law, by a court of competen t jurisdiction, by the rules of any stock exchange or by another appropriate regulatory body, provided that all reasonable steps to pr event such disclosure shall be taken, the disclosure shall be of the minimum amount re quired, and the Receiving Party consults the Disclosing Party first on the proposed form, timing, nature and purpose of the disclosure with as much prior notice as is practicable in the circumstances.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that each party must exercise the same degree of care as it applies to its own confidential information. The changed text adds a caveat that this doesn't require commercially unreasonable security measures. The contradicted text repeats the original obligation without the exception. The added caveat creates an in-text contradiction because it is now unclear whether the parties must implement security measures equivalent to their own, or whether they can avoid doing so if it's deemed commercially unreasonable. This contradicts the absolute requirement stated in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text:
Each party undertakes within three business days of the receipt of a written request of the other party or on termination of this Agreement, at the option of the other party:
(a) to return to the other party all of the other party's Confidential Information and that part of all documents and other material in its po ssession, custody or control that bear or incorporate any pa rt of the other pa rty's Confidential Information; or
(b) to destroy by shredding or incineration all of the other pa rty's Confidential Information and that part of all documents and other material in its possession, custody or control which bear or inco rporate any part of the other party's Confidential Information and take reason able steps to expung e all Confidential Information (and any copy that may have been ma de) from any computer, word processor or other device containing the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text:
Each party undertakes within three business days of the receipt of a written request of the other party or on termination of this Agreement, at the option of the other party:
(a) to return to the other party all of the other party's Confidential Information and that part of all documents and other material in its po ssession, custody or control that bear or incorporate any pa rt of the other pa rty's Confidential Information; or
(b) to destroy by shredding or incineration all of the other pa rty's Confidential Information and that part of all documents and other material in its possession, custody or control which bear or inco rporate any part of the other party's Confidential Information and take reason able steps to expung e all Confidential Information (and any copy that may have been ma de) from any computer, word processor or other device containing the Confidential Information. However, if the Confidential Information is stored electronically on a cloud service, the party may instead provide a written certification that the information is inaccessible.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction to how destroying confidential information should be handled. Initially, it is expected that there should be shredding or incineration or expunge all Confidential Information (and any copy that may have been made) from any computer, word processor or other device containing the Confidential Information. The new text says, that, if the Confidential Information is stored electronically on a cloud service, the party may instead provide a written certification that the information is inaccessible. This is a contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.1 Each party undertakes within three business days of the receipt of a written request of the other party or on termination of this Agreement, at the option of the other party: (a) to return to the other party all of the other party's Confidential Information and that part of all documents and other material in its po ssession, custody or control that bear or incorporate any pa rt of the other pa rty's Confidential Information; or (b) to destroy by shredding or incineration all of the other pa rty's Confidential Information and that part of all documents and other material in its possession, custody or control which bear or inco rporate any part of the other party's Confidential Information and take reason able steps to expung e all Confidential Information (and any copy that may have been ma de) from any computer, word processor or other device containing the Confidential Information. Any destruction of Confidential Information will be certified in writing to the other party by an authorised officer supervising it.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires shredding/incineration or expunging of confidential information from devices. The changed text introduces an exception for cloud services, allowing a written certification of inaccessibility instead. The contradicted text reiterates the original requirement for shredding/incineration or expunging, and mandates written certification of destruction, not merely inaccessibility, creating a clear and direct conflict between the clauses.

perturbation.original_text: Neither party shall disclose the Purpose or the existence of this Agreement or reveal that discussions are taking pla ce between the parties to another person without the prior written consent of the ot her party except to the extent that such disclosure is required by la w, by a court of co mpetent jurisdiction , by the rules of any stock exchange or by anothe r appropriate regulatory body.
perturbation.changed_text: Neither party shall disclose the Purpose or the existence of this Agreement or reveal that discussions are taking pla ce between the parties to another person without the prior written consent of the ot her party except to the extent that such disclosure is required by la w, by a court of co mpetent jurisdiction , by the rules of any stock exchange or by anothe r appropriate regulatory body. Notwithstanding the foregoing, either party may disclose the existence of this Agreement to potential investors or acquirers.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces an in-text contradiction by stating that, regardless of the prior statement, either party may disclose the existence of this Agreement to potential investors or acquirers, while it was expected for both parties to get written consent to disclose any of the terms of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9 Announcements Neither party shall disclose the Purpose or the existence of this Agreement or reveal that discussions are taking pla ce between the parties to another person without the prior written consent of the ot her party except to the extent that such disclosure is required by la w, by a court of co mpetent jurisdiction , by the rules of any stock exchange or by anothe r appropriate regulatory body.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that neither party shall disclose the existence of the agreement without prior written consent. The changed text adds an exception allowing either party to disclose the existence of the agreement to potential investors or acquirers, which directly contradicts the general prohibition stated in the original text and reiterated in the 'Contradicted Text' section. This constitutes a strong in-text contradiction because the changed text introduces an explicit exception to a previously absolute rule, thus meeting the definition of inconsistencies causing in-text contradictions, as the different sections of the contract now provide conflicting obligations regarding disclosure.

perturbation.original_text: Any claim for lo sses under this Agreement sha ll be restricted to direct loss only and shall not extend to indirect or cons equential loss.
perturbation.changed_text: Any claim for lo sses under this Agreement sha ll be restricted to direct loss only and shall not extend to indirect or cons equential loss. However, this limitation shall not apply to losses arising from a breach of Clause 2.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction to what kind of losses are applicable for a clam in the agreement. Initially, only direct losses were applicable, the change says, that this will not be applicable to losses arising from a breach of Clause 2. This could lead to disputes about whether losses are direct or consequential.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10.4 It is acknowledged that damages would no t be an adequate remedy for a breach of this Agreement and each party is entitled to the re medy of injunction, specific performance and other equitable relief for a threatened or actual breach of this Agreement. Any claim for lo sses under this Agreement sha ll be restricted to direct loss only and shall not extend to indirect or cons equential loss.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that 'Any claim for losses under this Agreement shall be restricted to direct loss only and shall not extend to indirect or consequential loss.' The changed text adds an exception: 'However, this limitation shall not apply to losses arising from a breach of Clause 2.' The contradicted text repeats the initial restriction to direct losses only. This creates a direct conflict because the changed text now allows for indirect or consequential losses in specific cases, while the contradicted text maintains the blanket restriction to direct losses. This adheres to the 'Inconsistencies' definition provided.
